title: uragoga subscandens
intro: Uragoga subscandens, also known as the Jamaican dogwood or biter bark, is a plant species native to the Caribbean and Central America, belonging to the family Melastomataceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, making it effective in treating various health conditions. Its medicinal properties include pain relief, fever reduction, and relief from digestive issues, among other health benefits. Uragoga subscandens can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture to harness its medicinal properties, although its usage should be approached with caution due to potential side effects and interactions with other medications.
